channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris works to energize Black male voters and denounces Trump support of 'stop and frisk'
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris warned Tuesday that Republican Donald Trump would try to “institutionalize” harsh policing tactics that disproportionately affect Black men nationwide as she promised to push for legislation to address discriminatory law enforcement practi
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: After decades in comedy, Tim Heidecker is ready to get serious about music
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tim Heidecker is working through his existential dread. “There’s a million things to think about as you’re trying to go to sleep at night,” he said. “ Post-pandemic, in the age of Trump, you know, North Korea, whatever it is.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors will not file criminal charges against 2 people at center of Los Angeles racism scandal
item.description: The Los Angeles city attorney’s office says it does not plan to file criminal charges against two people who were investigated in connection with the unlawful recording of a racist conversation that rocked City Hall in 2022 and prompted the city council president to resign after the audio was leaked
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: The Pentagon says more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Idaho will begin using deep veins as backup for lethal injection executions, officials say
item.description: Idaho will begin using central veins deep in the groin, neck, chest or arm for executions by lethal injection if attempts to insert standard IV lines fail.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-husband of 'Real Housewives' star gets seven years for hiring mobster to assault her boyfriend
item.description: The ex-husband of “Real Housewives of New Jersey” cast member Dina Manzo has been sentenced to seven years in prison for hiring a reputed mobster to assault her boyfriend in exchange for the defendant hosting a lavish wedding reception for the attacker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FEMA resumes door-to-door visits in North Carolina after threats tied to disinformation
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Federal disaster personnel have resumed door-to-door visits as part of their hurricane-recovery work in North Carolina, an effort temporarily suspended amid threats that prompted officials to condemn the spread of disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Apple Watch 10 proves itself as a medical device
item.description: Reportedly in the works for future versions of the Apple Watch: Blood pressure monitoring and blood glucose readings.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA resumes door-to-door visits in North Carolina after threats tied to disinformation
item.description: Officials say federal disaster workers have resumed door-to-door visits as part of hurricane recovery efforts in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family welcomes baby in Kalamazoo brewery parking lot
item.description: Mom Kyle Baker said the couple can't stop commenting on it: "We just had a baby in a car in front of One Well Brewery.
item.dc:creator: Josh Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Voters in California and Nevada consider ban on forced labor aimed at protecting prisoners
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California and Nevada voters will decide in November whether to ban forced prison labor by removing language from their state constitutions rooted in the legacy of chattel slavery.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Missouri, Halloween night signs were required at the homes of sex offenders. Until now
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Many states have laws designed to keep children away from convicted sexual offenders on Halloween night, such as curfews for those on offender registries and requirements to keep their porch lights off.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walgreens to close 1,200 stores across America
item.description: The plan announced Tuesday includes the closing of 300 stores that had been approved under a previous cost-cutting plan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two men shot during Pennsylvania assassination attempt on Trump say Secret Service failed them
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Two men who were shot during the first assassination attempt on Donald Trump this summer say the U.S. Secret Service was “negligent” in protecting the former president and other bystanders at the campaign rally in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new lawsuits, including man who was 16 at the time
item.description: Twice denied bail, the Bad Boy Records founder remains locked up a Brooklyn federal jail while awaiting trial in May.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. Robert Roberson, 57, is scheduled to receive a lethal injection on Thursday for the 2002 killing of his 2-year-old daughter, Nikki Curtis.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Aidan Hutchinson likely down 4-6 months with broken leg, Lions coach Dan Campbell says
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Aidan Hutchinson is likely done playing this year because of the leg injury he sustained against the Cowboys, but surgery to repair his fractured tibia and fibula “went great” and Lions coach Dan Campbell said Monday he isn't ready to declare his star pass rusher out for the
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Influx of homeless encampments under political signages seen across Las Vegas valley
item.description: Seeing political signage on almost every corner is nothing new during an election year, but what’s even more noticeable is the influx of unhoused people making shelter underneath.
item.dc:creator: Madison Kimbro, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, KRON4

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Nevada man with a shotgun, loaded handgun, ammunition and several fake passports in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump's rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert, authorities said. He was released the same day on $5,000 bail.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Historic Jersey Shore amusement park closes after generations of family thrills
item.description: OCEAN CITY, N.J. (AP) — For generations of vacationers heading to Ocean City, the towering “Giant Wheel” was the first thing they saw from miles away.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, ABC 4

item.title: Deputies 'probably' prevented 3rd Trump assassination attempt, California sheriff says
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: What is the purpose of an open-front toilet seat?
item.description: Open-front toilet seats — or “U-shaped” seats — are common in public bathrooms or commercial spaces, but they’re almost never the norm in private residences.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Sam's Club opening location in Texas, but there's no checkout lanes
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the warehouse club chain will soon open a store in Texas but there won't be any checkout lanes.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn't have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn't help but note the irony. The actor is being added to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame’s James F.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows mechanical SpaceX arms catching Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
